mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which reduces consumer spending expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy > 52% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer and residential investment demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of co-occurring up months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal accuracy drops below 50% during early-cycle recovery phases, or the 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO changes falls below 0.05 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index year-over-year growth surpasses 2.5 percent, inflation expectations rise and force markets to price in tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of threshold-crossing months, or the inflation threshold shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) over a 4-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where nonfarm payrolls decline while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously confirms a broad labor market contraction, which reduces aggregate demand and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series models, or forward returns remain positive in > 55% of divergence instances during recession onset phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly measures aggregate economic expansion, which increases corporate profit forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines while the federal funds rate rises, the combination of weakening supply-side output and tightening financial conditions creates a stagflationary pressure that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in > 55% of co-occurring instances during stagflationary environments.
